calendar.matters:
Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Jan-09-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Jacqueline Harvey, Esq. and David I. Holtzman, Esq. of HOLLAND & KNIGHT LLP, T:415.743.6977, for defendant/moving party;
Michael Martinovsky, Esq. of the LAW OFFICES OF MICHAEL MARTINOVSKY PC, T:415.230.5360, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. After arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT RICHARD MOORE'S Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs, as follows:

Defendant Richard Moore's motion for attorneys' fees and costs is denied.

In its complaint and first amended complaint, Plaintiff ORCA USA, Ltd. sought to state causes of action against Defendant Richard Moore for intentional interference with contractual relations and negligent interference with prospective economic relations in connection with Plaintiff's distribution and cooperation agreement with TG Digesters, Inc., a Canadian corporation. Those tort claims arose out of Moore's actions in negotiating a contract between Plaintiff and the Hyatt Centric Fisherman's Wharf Hotel. Plaintiff alleged that Moore was employed by the Hotel and was acting as its agent in taking those actions. (E.g., FAC 18, 24-28.) It did not assert any claims under the separate contract between Plaintiff and the Hotel, to which Moore is not a party. That contract provides, "The prevailing party in any action to enforce the provisions of this Agreement shall be paid all costs and expenses, including but not limited to reasonable attorneys' fees, incurred in connection with such action by the party which failed to perform its obligations under this Agreement." (Holtzman Decl., Ex. A.)

Civil Code section 1717(a) authorizes awards of attorneys' fees "[i]ny any action on a contract." "Whether attorney fees incurred in defending tort or other noncontract claims are recoverable after a pretrial dismissal depends upon the terms of the contractual attorney fee provision." (Santisas v. Goodin (1998) 17 Cal.4th 599, 602.) A narrow attorneys' fees clause such as the one involved here, which covers only actions "to enforce the contract," does not authorize recovery for services on tort or other claims. (Reynolds Metals Co. v. Alperson (1979) 25 Cal.3d 124, 129; Casella v. SouthWest Dealer Services, Inc. (2007) 157 Cal.App.4th 1127, 1161-1162 [narrow provision limiting fees to those "incurred in enforcing or attempting to enforce" employment agreement did not permit recovery of fees former employee incurred in prosecuting wrongful discharge tort claim against employer].) Thus, even if Moore could recover fees incurred by the Hotel in defending the action on his behalf, and even if Plaintiff had not voluntarily dismissed the action, the attorneys' fees clause in the agreement between ORCA and the Hotel is not broad enough to cover the claims in the action. "Such tort claims do not seek to enforce the . . . agreement." (Casella, 157 Cal.App.4th at 1162.)

Counsel for the plaintiff is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
